title: P2-series astromech droid

The '''P2-series astromech droid''' was [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton's]] first [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] prototype for the [[R-series/Legends|R-series]], using a rounded body design that would later prove popular with the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droids]].

==Description==
P2 units were prototype [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromechs]] standing 2.2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. They were equipped with three wheeled legs, a rotating head dome, three retractable [[Manipulator arm/Legends|manipulator arms]], a heavy [[Grasper arm/Legends|grasper arm]], [[Circular saw/Legends|buzzsaw]], and [[Laser welder/Legends|laser welder]] inside an [[Armor/Legends|armored]] housing.<ref name="CFTD" /> 

P2s had a primitive version of the [[Intellex ship-configuration computer]],<ref name="EGTD">''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> the [[Intellex II]],<ref name="J7" /> making them capable maintenance units aboard [[Starship/Legends|starships]] such as [[Bulk cruiser/Legends|bulk cruisers]] and [[Container ship/Legends|container ships]].<ref name="EGTD" /> [[Technician/Legends|Technicians]] also installed a primitive [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]] into the droid that gave it the ability to calculate a single [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump if given enough time and information.<ref name="J7" />

The droids were large, cumbersome, clumsy, sluggish, and slow to learn,<ref name="EGTD" /> although they had a cheerful and positive attitude.<ref name="AEG" /> They could only communicate through the use of its [[Viewscreen/Legends|video display screen]] or by linking to a [[Computer/Legends|computer]]. Despite these drawbacks, many of the features including in the P2-series later became common on astromechs in [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton's]] [[R-series/Legends|R-series]].<ref name="EGTD" />

==Role==
The P2 was an astromech, but was also intended by Industrial Automaton to be the first in a new kind of droid. The unit was designed to be a versatile mechanic that could perform a wide variety of functions. The P2 was described as a "walking toolbox," a nickname that would also be applied to its successor, the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series]].<ref name="FF21">{{FactFile|21|AST 1-2|P2 and R1 Astromech Droids}}</ref>

==History==
[[File:P2_OSWFF2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Schematics for a P2-series [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]].]]
As a prototype, Industrial Automaton sold P2s on a trial basis exclusively to the [[Old Republic shipping fleet]]. Although the P2s had their issues, they proved popular with the [[Captain/Legends|captains]] of the ships they were stationed on. Industrial Automaton was in the process of finalizing plans to make the P2-series publicly available when the company ran into trouble.<ref name="EGTD" /> 

The Intellex computer used in the P2 had been developed by [[Industrial Intelligence/Legends|Industrial Intelligence]] before their hostile takeover by [[Automata Galactica/Legends|Automata Galactica]] to form Industrial Automaton. Former executives from Industrial Intelligence launched legal action against the board of Industrial Automaton for use of the system they developed. Due to the state of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]] at the time, the case was frozen for [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] before it ever went to trial. Production of the P2 was stalled, and Industrial Automaton lost millions of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] in cancelled preorders.<ref name="J7" />

The former employees of Industrial Intelligence eventually dropped the case when it became clear that the lawsuit would lay bare [[Datafile/Legends|datafiles]] and business practices that they would rather keep secret.<ref name="J7" /> The damage was done however. The legal delays and bad publicity around the case killed the P2 line and forced Industrial Automaton to start over.<ref name="EGTD" /> Industrial Automaton designed a replacement model, the [[R1-series astromech droid/Legends|R1 unit]], by recycling the shells of existing [[Mark II reactor drone/Legends|Mark II reactor drones]] to save costs that had been incurred by the company's legal battles.<ref name="EGTD" />

By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], any P2s still in operation were commonly found on independent or [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] vessels.<ref name="J7" /> One P2 droid, [[P2-D19]], was stationed aboard the ''[[Black Ice/Legends|Black Ice]]''. When a team of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] [[Agent/Legends|agents]] attempted to overtake the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] supply ship, [[Chief engineer/Legends|chief engineer]] [[Skolos]] reprogrammed the droid to attack the intruders.<ref name="BI">''[[Black Ice (adventure)|Black Ice]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
For the P2 droid's first appearance in the [[roleplaying game]] game supplement, [[Black Ice (adventure)|''Black Ice'']], artist Bob Dvorak based his illustration on early [[Ralph McQuarrie]] concept art of [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]].<ref name="Databank">{{DB|droids|p2prototype|P2 prototype astromech droid}}</ref>

==Appearances==
